why: Both are build-tool advisories that do NOT reach the static output served to users (D-154, D-149-style analysis). The vite fix is a breaking 5->8 major bump, deferred to a post-launch branch. This acknowledgement expires so the deferral cannot be forgotten; a NEW advisory still fails the gate.

advisories.GHSA-fx2h-pf6j-xcff: vite dev-server `server.fs.deny` bypass on Windows alternate paths. Affects `vite dev`/`vite preview` only, never the built static assets. Fix is vite@8 (breaking). Deferred; dev-machine/pipeline risk only.
advisories.GHSA-v2hh-gcrm-f6hx: fast-uri host confusion. Build-time transitive: vite-plugin-pwa -> workbox-build -> ajv -> fast-uri, used to validate our OWN workbox config during `vite build`. Not in the shipped runtime bundle.
